methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $1.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 8 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $1.0B - $8.0B.

methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 42.9x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Apex Space is a Series D frontier-tech company in Space & Aerospace founded by Ian Cinnamon and Max Benassi, currently valued at $1.0B+ post-money and having raised $518M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 8 same-sector private peers at comparable or adjacent stages, alongside 5 public Space & Aerospace tickers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) that anchor sector multiples.

Two quantitative methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.5x multiplier to the $1.0B last post-money valuation, implying $1.5B. Peer-median analysis across 8 same-sector peers—including Sierra Space ($8.0B, Series C), Astranis ($3.5B, Series D), ICEYE ($2.8B, Series E), Varda Space Industries ($1.5B, Series C), and Skyryse ($1.1B, Series C)—yields a median of $1.3B, with a range of $1.0B to $8.0B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied because Apex Space's revenue is not on file; the sector P/S median is 42.9x across public comparables, but remains unusable absent revenue disclosure.

Triangulation of the two applicable methods supports a valuation range of $1.1B (low) to $1.7B (high), with a median of $1.4B. The primary uncertainty is stage-appropriate step-up magnitude; peer dispersion ($1.0B–$8.0B) reflects heterogeneity in business model, funding stage, and revenue traction within frontier aerospace. Revenue-based valuation would significantly narrow this range.
